L. 104–193section 601 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: For each of fiscal years 2017 and 2018, the Secretary shall pay to each Indian tribe that has an approved tribal family assistance plan a tribal family assistance grant for the fiscal year in an amount equal to the amount determined under subparagraph (B), which shall be reduced for a fiscal year, on a pro rata basis for each quarter, in the case of a tribal family assistance plan approved during a fiscal year for which the plan is to be in effect, and shall reduce the grant payable under to any State in which lies the service area or areas of the Indian tribe by that portion of the amount so determined that is attributable to expenditures by the State.section 603(a)(1) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The amount determined under this subparagraph is an amount equal to the total amount of the Federal payments to a State or States under (as in effect during such fiscal year) for fiscal year 1994 attributable to expenditures (other than child care expenditures) by the State or States under parts A and F (as so in effect) for fiscal year 1994 for Indian families residing in the service area or areas identified by the Indian tribe pursuant to subsection (b)(1)(C) of this section.section 603 of this title

children.heading: Use of State submitted data
children.label: ii Use of State submitted data

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: The Secretary shall use State submitted data to make each determination under clause (i).

children.heading: Disagreement with determination
children.label: II Disagreement with determination
children.text: If an Indian tribe or tribal organization disagrees with State submitted data described under subclause (I), the Indian tribe or tribal organization may submit to the Secretary such additional information as may be relevant to making the determination under clause (i) and the Secretary may consider such information before making such determination.

children.heading: Grants for Indian tribes that received jobs funds
children.label: 2 Grants for Indian tribes that received jobs funds

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: For each of fiscal years 2017 and 2018, the Secretary shall pay to each eligible Indian tribe that proposes to operate a program described in subparagraph (C) a grant in an amount equal to the amount received by the Indian tribe in fiscal year 1994 under (as in effect during fiscal year 1994).section 682(i) of this title

children.heading: Eligible Indian tribe
children.label: B Eligible Indian tribe
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), the term “eligible Indian tribe” means an Indian tribe or Alaska Native organization that conducted a job opportunities and basic skills training program in fiscal year 1995 under (as in effect during fiscal year 1995).section 682(i) of this title

children.heading: Use of grant
children.label: C Use of grant
children.text: Each Indian tribe to which a grant is made under this paragraph shall use the grant for the purpose of operating a program to make work activities available to such population and such service area or areas as the tribe specifies.

children.heading: Appropriation
children.label: D Appropriation
children.text: Out of any money in the Treasury of the United States not otherwise appropriated, there are appropriated $7,633,287 for each fiscal year specified in subparagraph (A) for grants under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary of Labor shall award a grant in accordance with this paragraph to an Indian tribe for each fiscal year specified in for which the Indian tribe is a welfare-to-work tribe, in such amount as the Secretary of Labor deems appropriate, subject to subparagraph (B) of this paragraph.section 603(a)(5)(H) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: The Indian tribe has submitted to the Secretary of Labor a plan which describes how, consistent with , the Indian tribe will use any funds provided under this paragraph during the fiscal year. If the Indian tribe has a tribal family assistance plan, the plan referred to in the preceding sentence shall be in the form of an addendum to the tribal family assistance plan.section 603(a)(5) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: The Indian tribe is operating a program under a tribal family assistance plan approved by the Secretary of Health and Human Services, a program described in paragraph (2)(C), or an employment program funded through other sources under which substantial services are provided to recipients of assistance under a program funded under this part.

children.label: iii
children.text: The Indian tribe has provided the Secretary of Labor with an estimate of the amount that the Indian tribe intends to expend during the fiscal year (excluding tribal expenditures described in section 609(a)(7)(B)(iv) (other than subclause (III) thereof) of this title) pursuant to this paragraph.

children.label: iv
children.text: The Indian tribe has agreed to negotiate in good faith with the Secretary of Health and Human Services with respect to the substance and funding of any evaluation under , and to cooperate with the conduct of any such evaluation.section 613(j) of this title

children.heading: Limitations on use of funds
children.label: C Limitations on use of funds

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: shall apply to funds provided to Indian tribes under this paragraph in the same manner in which such section applies to funds provided under .Section 603(a)(5)(C) of this titlesection 603(a)(5) of this title

children.heading: Waiver authority
children.label: ii Waiver authority
children.text: The Secretary of Labor may waive or modify the application of a provision of section 603(a)(5)(C) (other than clause (viii) thereof) of this title with respect to an Indian tribe to the extent necessary to enable the Indian tribe to operate a more efficient or effective program with the funds provided under this paragraph.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: iii Regulations
children.text: Within 90 days after , the Secretary of Labor, after consultation with the Secretary of Health and Human Services and the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development, shall prescribe such regulations as may be necessary to implement this paragraph.August 5, 1997

children.label: A
children.text: outlines the Indian tribe’s approach to providing welfare-related services for the 3-year period, consistent with this section;

children.label: B
children.text: specifies whether the welfare-related services provided under the plan will be provided by the Indian tribe or through agreements, contracts, or compacts with intertribal consortia, States, or other entities;

children.label: C
children.text: identifies the population and service area or areas to be served by such plan;

children.label: D
children.text: provides that a family receiving assistance under the plan may not receive duplicative assistance from other State or tribal programs funded under this part;

children.label: E
children.text: identifies the employment opportunities in or near the service area or areas of the Indian tribe and the manner in which the Indian tribe will cooperate and participate in enhancing such opportunities for recipients of assistance under the plan consistent with any applicable State standards; and

children.label: F
children.text: applies the fiscal accountability provisions of section 5(f)(1) of the Indian Self-Determination and Education Assistance Act (), relating to the submission of a single-agency audit report required by chapter 75 of title 31.25 U.S.C. 450c(f)(1)1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Approval
children.label: 2 Approval
children.text: The Secretary shall approve each tribal family assistance plan submitted in accordance with paragraph (1).

children.heading: Consortium of tribes
children.label: 3 Consortium of tribes
children.text: Nothing in this section shall preclude the development and submission of a single tribal family assistance plan by the participating Indian tribes of an intertribal consortium.

children.heading: Minimum work participation requirements and time limits
children.label: c Minimum work participation requirements and time limits

children.text: consistent with the purposes of this section;
consistent with the economic conditions and resources available to each tribe; and
similar to comparable provisions in .section 607(e) of this title

children.heading: Emergency assistance
children.label: d Emergency assistance
children.text: Nothing in this section shall preclude an Indian tribe from seeking emergency assistance from any Federal loan program or emergency fund.
generally accepted accounting principles; and
the requirements of the Indian Self-Determination and Education Assistance Act ( et seq.).25 U.S.C. 4501

children.heading: Eligibility for Federal loans
children.label: f Eligibility for Federal loans
children.text: shall apply to an Indian tribe with an approved tribal assistance plan in the same manner as such section applies to a State, except that shall be applied by substituting “section 612(a)” for “section 603(a)”.Section 606 of this titlesection 606(c) of this title
Subsections (a)(1), (a)(6), (b), and (c) of , shall apply to an Indian tribe with an approved tribal assistance plan in the same manner as such subsections apply to a State.section 609 of this title

shall apply to an Indian tribe with an approved tribal assistance plan by substituting “meet minimum work participation requirements established under ” for “comply with ”.Section 609(a)(3) of this titlesection 612(c) of this titlesection 607(a) of this title

children.heading: Data collection and reporting
children.label: h Data collection and reporting
children.text: shall apply to an Indian tribe with an approved tribal family assistance plan.Section 611 of this title

children.heading: Special rule for Indian tribes in Alaska
children.label: i Special rule for Indian tribes in Alaska

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this section, and except as provided in paragraph (2), an Indian tribe in the State of Alaska that receives a tribal family assistance grant under this section shall use the grant to operate a program in accordance with requirements comparable to the requirements applicable to the program of the State of Alaska funded under this part. Comparability of programs shall be established on the basis of program criteria developed by the Secretary in consultation with the State of Alaska and such Indian tribes.

children.heading: Waiver
children.label: 2 Waiver
children.text: An Indian tribe described in paragraph (1) may apply to the appropriate State authority to receive a waiver of the requirement of paragraph (1).
